user_story: As a discovery/scan operator, I want the system to pick the best-fitting credential for a target host so that I can authenticate without manually choosing among many stored credentials.
expected_behavior: Body is validated against credentialMatchContextSchema, then the controller calls credentialService.findBestMatch(context). The affinity service loads all credentials (optionally filtered by required_protocol/required_scope in SQL), decrypts each, and scores them: CIDR network +30, hostname glob +25, OS +20, device +15, environment +10, cloud provider +20, priority*2 bonus, or base score 1 when nothing matches; the top-scoring credential is returned with its score and reasons, secrets redacted to '***REDACTED***'. Returns 200 with data, 404 when no credential matches, 500 on error.
issue_note: credentialMatchContextSchema (unified-credential.routes.ts:95-104) declares underscore-prefixed keys (_ip,_hostname,_required_protocol,...) but the affinity service reads un-prefixed context.ip/hostname/required_protocol (affinity.service.ts:41-49,133-196); since validate() uses stripUnknown:true (common validators.ts:36) the real context fields are stripped, so match always runs with an empty context and ignores affinity entirely.

feature: Rank all credentials by match score
user_story: As an automation engineer, I want a ranked list of candidate credentials for a context so that I can try them in order of likelihood.
expected_behavior: Body is validated against credentialMatchContextSchema, then rankCredentials(context) returns every credential scored by the same affinity algorithm and sorted by score descending, each with score and human-readable reasons and secrets redacted. Responds 200 with the data array plus _count; 500 on error.
issue_note: Same root cause as match: credentialMatchContextSchema's underscore-prefixed keys (routes:95-104) + stripUnknown:true (validators.ts:36) strip the caller's context, so rank runs with an empty context and every credential collapses to the default base score of 1 (affinity.service.ts:206-208).

user_story: As an administrator, I want to browse and filter stored credentials without exposing secrets so that I can manage the credential inventory.
expected_behavior: Optional query is validated; the controller builds filters (protocol, scope, tags split on commas, created_by, limit, offset) and calls credentialService.list, returning UnifiedCredentialSummary rows that exclude all secret material. Responds 200 with data and _count; 500 on error.
issue_note: listCredentialsQuerySchema (routes:109-116) uses underscore-prefixed keys (_protocol,_scope,_limit,...) while the controller reads un-prefixed req.query['protocol']/['limit'] etc (controller:82-89); with stripUnknown:true the real query params are stripped (and only _limit/_offset defaults injected), so every list filter is silently ignored.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to store a credential for a given auth protocol so that scans and integrations can authenticate to targets.
expected_behavior: Body is validated (name, protocol, scope, and credentials object required; protocol/scope restricted to enum values). The CRUD service encrypts the credentials object with AES-256-GCM into an {iv,encryptedData,authTag} envelope (crud.service.ts:67) stored in credentials.credentials, and persists protocol, scope, affinity, tags and created_by (taken from req.user?.id or 'system'). Returns 201 with the credential and secrets redacted; 409 on duplicate name (Postgres 23505); 500 otherwise.
issue_note: The nested affinity is validated by credentialAffinitySchema (routes:58-66) whose keys are underscore-prefixed (_networks,_hostname_patterns,_priority,...) but the CredentialAffinity type and affinity scoring use un-prefixed networks/priority (affinity.service.ts:133-203); with stripUnknown:true the affinity sub-fields are stripped, so submitted affinity rules are silently discarded and stored as {}.

user_story: As a user connecting an OAuth2 credential, I want the provider's redirect to complete the connection so that the credential gains usable tokens.
expected_behavior: Reads error/state/code query params: returns 400 if the provider returned an error or if state/code are missing/empty. Otherwise calls getOAuthSubstrate(pool).handleCallback({state,code}), which exchanges the code and persists the sealed token bundle (bundle_ciphertext) into credentials.oauth_metadata via CmdbOAuthCredentialStore (plaintext tokens are never stored). Responds 200 with {source_id, connected:true}; 400 when the OAuth state is invalid/expired; 500 otherwise. Route is registered before /credentials/:id so the literal path is not captured as an id (routes:195-198).

user_story: As an administrator, I want to retrieve a single credential's metadata so that I can inspect its configuration.
expected_behavior: Validates id presence (400 if absent). The CRUD service decrypts the stored envelope via decodeStoredCredentials (with a legacy-plaintext fallback when the column is not an encryption envelope, crud.service.ts:34-42), but the controller redacts the secret material to '***REDACTED***' before responding 200. Returns 404 when not found, 500 on error. The handler's doc comment notes the decrypted payload should be admin-restricted, but no authorization check is enforced at this layer (createdBy/user come from req.user with a 'system' fallback).

user_story: As an administrator, I want to update a credential's name, secrets, affinity or tags so that I can keep it current.
expected_behavior: Validates id and body (name/description/credentials/affinity/tags all optional). The service updates only the provided fields, re-encrypting the credentials object with AES-256-GCM when supplied. Returns 200 with secrets redacted; 404 when not found, 409 on duplicate name, 500 otherwise.
issue_note: Like create, the nested affinity uses credentialAffinitySchema's underscore-prefixed keys (routes:58-66, referenced at line 88); with stripUnknown:true any affinity object sent on update is emptied before reaching the service, so affinity rules cannot be updated via REST.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to delete a credential so that obsolete or compromised secrets are removed.
expected_behavior: Validates id presence (400 if absent), then calls credentialService.delete(id) and responds 204 No Content on success. Returns 404 when the credential is not found and 409 when it is currently used (e.g. referenced by a credential set or resource); 500 otherwise.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to validate that a credential is well-formed for its protocol so that I catch misconfiguration before a scan uses it.
expected_behavior: Validates id presence, then the validation service loads and decrypts the credential and runs protocol-specific structure checks (validateCredentialStructure), persists validation_status ('valid'/'invalid') and last_validated_at to the DB, and returns the CredentialValidationResult. Responds 200 on success. Note: this checks credential shape only, not a live connection; and unlike sibling endpoints the handler has no not-found branch, so validating a non-existent id surfaces as a generic 500 rather than 404.

user_story: As a user, I want to start the OAuth2 authorization flow for a credential so that I can grant the app access and obtain tokens.
expected_behavior: Defaults provider to SERVICENOW_PROVIDER_ID when the body omits a non-empty provider; validates that scopes (when present) is an array of strings (400 otherwise). Loads the credential (404 if missing) and requires protocol === 'oauth2' (400 otherwise). Calls getOAuthSubstrate(pool).authorizationUrl({sourceId:id, providerId, scopes}) and returns 200 with {authorization_url, state}; 500 on error. This route has no Joi validateRequest middleware — provider/scopes are validated inline in the handler.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to list credential sets with their member credentials so that I can review grouped fallback/rotation strategies.
expected_behavior: Calls credentialSetService.list(), which reads the credential_set_summaries view and returns each set with expanded credential summaries (no secrets), its strategy, stop_on_success, tags and usage_count, ordered by created_at desc. Responds 200 with data and _count; 500 on error.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to group several credentials into a set with a try-strategy so that scans can attempt them in order (e.g. admin, then root, then service account).
expected_behavior: Intended behavior: validate the body (name + non-empty credential_ids array of UUIDs required; strategy defaulting to 'sequential', stop_on_success defaulting to true), verify every credential_id exists (400 'do not exist' with the missing ids otherwise), insert into credential_sets, and return 201 with the created set; 409 on duplicate name.
issue_note: createCredentialSetSchema (routes:121-128) declares its required keys with underscore prefixes (_name, _credential_ids) but real bodies and CredentialSetInput use un-prefixed name/credential_ids; with stripUnknown:true and abortEarly:false (validators.ts:36) the required _name/_credential_ids are always absent from the input, so POST /credential-sets returns 400 Validation Error for every request, making the endpoint effectively unusable.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to fetch a single credential set with its expanded credentials so that I can inspect its membership and strategy.
expected_behavior: Validates id presence (400 if absent). Calls getWithCredentials(id), which reads the credential_set_summaries view and returns the set plus expanded credential summaries (no secrets) and usage_count. Responds 200 on success, 404 when not found, 500 on error.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to change a credential set's members, strategy or metadata so that I can keep the grouping accurate.
expected_behavior: Intended behavior: validate id and body (name/credential_ids/strategy/stop_on_success/tags all optional, strategy restricted to sequential|parallel|adaptive), update the provided fields, and return 200 with the updated set; 404 not found, 409 duplicate name, 400 when supplied credential_ids do not exist.
issue_note: updateCredentialSetSchema (routes:133-140) uses underscore-prefixed keys (_name,_credential_ids,_strategy,...) while the service reads un-prefixed input fields; with stripUnknown:true every submitted field is stripped before reaching the controller, so the update forwards an empty object and PUT /credential-sets silently changes nothing.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to delete a credential set so that obsolete groupings are removed.
expected_behavior: Validates id presence (400 if absent), then calls credentialSetService.delete(id) and responds 204 No Content on success. Returns 404 when not found and 409 when the set is currently used; 500 otherwise.

user_story: As an automation engineer, I want an ordered list of a set's credentials for a given target context so that I can attempt authentication in the optimal order.
expected_behavior: Reads body.context and optional body.strategy override. selectCredentials loads the set (404 if missing), fetches and decrypts its member credentials, then orders them: 'sequential' and 'parallel' preserve the set's credential_ids order, while 'adaptive' ranks credentials by affinity score against the context and falls back to set order on ties. Returns the ordered credentials with secrets redacted, 200 with _count; 500 on error.
issue_note: selectCredentialsSchema (routes:145-148) uses _context/_strategy but the controller reads req.body.context/req.body.strategy (controller:269-270); with stripUnknown:true both are stripped, so select always runs with an empty context and the set's default strategy, meaning the adaptive strategy's affinity matching never receives the caller's context.
